papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: The paper evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than cognition in human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The study develops speech-based dementia biomarkers from human clinical corpora but does not analyze an interactive decision task, eye tracking, EEG connectivity, or a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: The paper investigates reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: The perspective proposes games, eye tracking, and behavioral timing for studying human executive function but reports no participant study that measures or analyzes these elements.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: Human digit-span performance and pupil dynamics are central to cognitive-load classification, while the EEG analysis uses generic time-series features rather than frequency-domain connectivity.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: The paper concerns KV-cache memory and quantization in autoregressive video generation models rather than human participants.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: The study monitors human cognitive load from EEG but provides no interactive-task methodology or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: The human working-memory task supplies interactive behavioral context, but EEG is reduced to band-power and generic features and the synthetic model is not a cognitive decision-process model.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: The working-memory paradigm provides an active human task, but the primary method maps ECG features to EEG band summaries rather than analyzing EEG connectivity or a cognitive generative model.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: The study combines adaptive VR attention and arithmetic tasks with eye tracking as a central input for real-time cognitive-load estimation.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: Participants perform recall and sequence working-memory tasks while beta-phase EEG connectivity and regional synchrony are analyzed as primary biomarkers.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: The modelling includes animal behavior and validation against macaque neural data, so the study population is not restricted to human participants.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: The study includes an active working-memory condition and analyzes EEG connectivity and cross-frequency phase-amplitude coupling as sleep-quality biomarkers.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: Human eye tracking is central to modeling dyslexic reading costs, but naturalistic reading is not an interactive sequential-decision task and no EEG or cognitive generative model is used.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: Human working-memory encoding and retrieval tasks are paired with phase-lag and entropy-based EEG functional-connectivity analysis for AD, MCI, and control classification.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: The evaluation uses simulated sessions and synthetic test-item trajectories rather than a study population of human participants.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: The paper reviews human EEG research on attention and learning but conducts no participant-level task, eye-tracking, connectivity, or cognitive-process modelling analysis.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: The healthy-volunteer pilot centrally measures frequency-tagged auditory and visual steady-state EEG responses, but its paradigms are passive and include no behavioral or eye-tracking component.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: Participants actively navigate landmark-based VR routes, but the reported EEG methodology emphasizes classification accuracy without qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: The paper presents a theoretical neural-circuit model without a human participant study population.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: The human protocol assesses cognitive load and stress, but the task is insufficiently described as interactive and EEG results are limited to band-level and machine-learned features.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: The study predicts executive-function test scores from passively recorded sleep EEG and ECG using deep learning without an analyzed interactive task, connectivity method, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The review addresses human dementia assessment broadly but contains none of the specified behavioral, eye-tracking, EEG, or computational-modelling methodologies.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: The method uses frequency-tagged FPVS EEG as a central biomarker of cognitive impairment, but stimulation is explicitly passive and no behavioral interaction or eye tracking is analyzed.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: Participants complete sequential working-memory and reaction-time assessments in VR and on a PC, but the study includes no eye tracking, EEG analysis, or cognitive generative model.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: The paper benchmarks cognitive abilities and executive functions in LLMs rather than studying human participants.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: A clinical human sample completes interactive executive-function minigames with continuous behavioral and gaze-trajectory analysis for psychometric assessment.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: Participants navigate VR mazes while performing attention and working-memory tasks, with eye tracking and continuous behavior recorded and analyzed as important predictive features.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: The model is evaluated on human panoramic eye-tracking datasets, but viewing is passive and the Transformer is a predictive model rather than a generative model of human decision processes.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: The human safety study co-records EEG and executive-function outcomes but does not describe an interactive task or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.
